questions.question: What does `display: grid` create?
options: A grid container with grid formatting context
questions.explanation: `display: grid` creates a grid container, establishing a grid formatting context for its children.

questions.question: How many dimensions does CSS Grid work in?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid is two-dimensional, allowing simultaneous control of rows and columns.

questions.question: What is the `fr` unit?
questions.explanation: `fr` stands for fraction — it distributes available space proportionally.

questions.question: Which property defines the columns of a grid?
questions.explanation: `grid-template-columns` defines the number and size of grid columns.

questions.question: How is a grid item placed in a named grid area?
questions.explanation: Use `grid-area: name` on the item to assign it to a named area from `grid-template-areas`.

questions.question: What is the difference between CSS Grid and Flexbox?
options: Grid is two-dimensional; Flexbox is one-dimensional
Grid is newer so it replaces Flexbox
Flexbox is always better for any layout
questions.explanation: Grid handles rows and columns simultaneously, while Flexbox works along one axis at a time.

questions.question: Which property defines the rows of a grid?
questions.explanation: `grid-template-rows` defines the number and size of grid rows.

questions.question: What does `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr` create?
questions.explanation: Each `1fr` is one fraction, creating three columns that each take 1/3 of the available space.

questions.question: What is a grid cell?
options: The intersection of a grid row and column
questions.explanation: A grid cell is the smallest unit in a grid — the intersection of one row and one column.

questions.question: What are grid lines?
options: The dividing lines that form the grid structure
questions.explanation: Grid lines are the numbered lines between grid tracks (columns and rows) used for placement.

questions.question: How do you set gaps between grid items?
questions.explanation: The `gap` property (and `row-gap`/`column-gap`) sets spacing between grid tracks.

questions.question: What is the repeat() function used for in Grid?
options: Creating multiple tracks with a repeated pattern
questions.explanation: `repeat(3, 1fr)` is equivalent to `1fr 1fr 1fr` — a shorthand for repeated track sizes.

questions.question: What does grid-template-areas do?
options: Defines named regions in the grid layout
Sets the area of each grid item
questions.explanation: `grid-template-areas` uses string notation to define named layout regions like header, sidebar, main.

questions.question: What is an explicit grid?
options: The grid defined by grid-template-columns and grid-template-rows
questions.explanation: The explicit grid is created by the grid-template-* properties. Items placed outside create an implicit grid.

questions.question: What does `grid-column: 1 / 3` do?
options: Makes an item span from column line 1 to column line 3
Sets the item to column 1 and 3
questions.explanation: `grid-column: 1 / 3` makes the item start at grid line 1 and end at line 3, spanning 2 columns.

questions.question: What is the difference between grid lines and grid tracks?
options: Lines are the dividers; tracks are the spaces between lines
Tracks are the lines; lines are the spaces
questions.explanation: Grid lines are the numbered dividers (1, 2, 3...), and tracks are the rows/columns between them.

questions.question: What property places a grid item by specifying start and end lines?
questions.explanation: Use `grid-column: start / end` and `grid-row: start / end` to place items by line numbers.

questions.question: What happens to children of a grid container by default?
options: They are placed automatically in grid cells
They stack on top of each other
questions.explanation: Grid items are automatically placed into grid cells following the row direction by default.

questions.question: What is the default alignment of grid items?
questions.explanation: Grid items stretch to fill their assigned cell by default (align-items and justify-items default to stretch).

questions.question: Can you nest a Flexbox container inside a Grid item?
options: Yes, Grid items can contain any layout including Flexbox
No, Grid items must only contain grid items
questions.explanation: Grid items are regular block elements that can contain any other layout method, including Flexbox.
